note.soap:
S:  
Patient called with an unspecified complaint. No additional symptom details or history were provided. No known medication allergies reported. No other clinical information available.

O:  
No physical exam findings available due to phone-only encounter. No vital signs or objective data provided.

A:  
- Working diagnosis (most likely): Unable to determine due to lack of clinical information.  
- Differential: Cannot be established without symptom details.  
- Red flags considered: None identified due to absence of symptom data.

P:  
- Disposition: Recommend patient seek in-person evaluation if symptoms develop or worsen, or if urgent concerns arise.  
- Plan: Await further information or symptom description to guide diagnosis and management. May be appropriate to follow up with primary care or urgent care for evaluation. Reviewing physician will confirm and prescribe if indicated.  
- Safety net: Advise patient to call or present urgently if experiencing new or worsening symptoms such as severe pain, difficulty breathing, chest pain, altered mental status, high fever, or other concerning signs.
patient_instructions: If you are not improving as expected, or if symptoms worsen, seek in-person medical care.
Medication options you may consider if safe (no dosing by phone): acetaminophen (Tylenol) if you can take it safely; ibuprofen (Advil) if you can take it safely.
